author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
content:
DISCHARGE SUMMARY — UNIVERSITY OF MARYLAND MEDICAL CENTER
Admission 7/6/2027 — Discharge 7/11/2027.

ADMISSION DIAGNOSES: Acute segmental PE (low-risk); extensive right LE DVT; incidental liver mass concerning for malignancy.

HOSPITAL COURSE: Previously healthy 43-year-old man with acute unprovoked PE and extensive DVT, started on a heparin infusion with prompt symptomatic improvement and no right-heart strain. Dedicated liver MRI and triple-phase CT characterized a large right-hepatic-lobe lesion with left-lobe foci and a right portal-vein thrombus, with high radiologic suspicion for malignancy and a recommendation to biopsy. Hematology, interventional radiology, and hepatology were involved. He was transitioned from heparin to apixaban and remained stable.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES: Acute VTE (PE + DVT), on anticoagulation; liver mass — tissue diagnosis pending.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS: Apixaban (acute-VTE regimen). Acetaminophen PRN. (No NSAIDs.)

FOLLOW-UP:
- Interventional radiology: OUTPATIENT CT-guided liver biopsy (to be scheduled).
- Hepatology and hematology: outpatient follow-up.
- Strict return precautions: recurrent chest pain, dyspnea, leg swelling, bleeding.

The importance of adherence to anticoagulation and of prompt re-evaluation for any recurrent symptoms was emphasized.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Daniel Okafor, MD (PGY-2) and Dr. Marisol Vega, MD. 7/11/2027.
